title: uruguayan padron peppers empanadas
description: fluffy, garlicky empanadas filled with uruguayan-style padron peppers, perfect for a festive meal

ingredients.name: wet curd cheese
ingredients.notes: such as queso fresco or ricotta

ingredients.name: green chili peppers
ingredients.notes: such as padron or piri piri

instructions: mix the flour and salt in a bowl.
add the cold butter and use a pastry blender to mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
add the wet curd cheese and mix until the dough begins to form.
divide the dough in half and roll each half into a thin sheet.
cut the sheet into circles using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
place a spoonful of the padron pepper mixture in the center of each circle.
brush the edges with the beaten egg and fold the empanada in half.
press the edges together to seal the empanada.
place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with olive oil.
bake for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown.
if using ghee, brush the tops of the empanadas with ghee during the last 5 minutes of baking.
